name: Coquilles St. Jacques Au Gingembre (Scallops with Ginger)
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Unsalted butter, chilled, cut into small pieces
2 Green onions - sliced -OR- 1 small Leek, thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Roll out puff pastry into a 10-inch circle that is 1/4 inch thick. Place on baking sheet and cut out an 8-inch circle from the center, discarding excess pastry. Brush with egg.
3. Cut concentric circles 1 inch from the edge and halfway through the pastry. Chill pastry for 10 minutes.
4. Bake at 450°F for 15 minutes. Reduce heat to 350°F and continue baking until pastry is golden brown, about 15 to 20 minutes. Return oven temperature to 450°F.
5. Carefully lift off the pastry cover formed by the concentric circles and gently remove the moist dough inside the shell.
6. In a medium saucepan, combine wine, shallot, and ginger. Bring to boil over medium heat. Add scallops and cream and cook for 1 to 2 minutes. Remove scallops using a slotted spoon. Increase heat to medium-high and cook the liquid until reduced to 2 tablespoons, adding any juices drained from scallops.
7. Whisk in butter one piece at a time. Season with salt and pepper. Add lemon juice. Set sauce aside.
8. Melt remaining butter in a small skillet over high heat. Add green onion and cook for 30 seconds. Set aside.
9. Stir scallops into the sauce and heat through. Reheat pastry shell and lid in the oven for 2 minutes.
10. To serve, place the pastry shell on a heated platter and fill with scallop mixture. Sprinkle with green onion and top with pastry lid.
